site.name: oliverbrown.design
site.title: oliverbrown.design
site.description: Oliver Brown builds targeted EdTech that makes learners into doers and lets teachers be teachers.

about.heading: about
blocks: 11 years designing playful, hands-on learning in the classroom taught me what rich learning experiences look like and today's EdTech has a long way to go. After years of waiting for truly innovative digital learning to arrive on my students' laptops, I decided to go build it myself.

I got a master's in EdTech from Harvard and a great job designing learning games. Since then I've led the design of 27 innovative games, designed a suite of 9 best-in-class accessibility features, reached more than 2 million K–12 students, and supported 174,000+ educators in 7,400+ schools.

pieces.title: TREASURE BOX
pieces.subtitle: A suite of best-in-class math games that reinvent place value learning
overview: Treasure Box is a suite of 6 learning games I designed for ST Math. Going beyond math tools that merely quiz students on place value, carrying, and borrowing as abstract concepts, Treasure Box lets students concretely compose, decompose, and regroup numbers with unique digital manipulatives designed from the ground up to teach these concepts. Spanning 3rd–6th grade Common Core and Texas standards, it remains the largest game development effort in ST Math's history, and is currently played by more than a million students every school year.

pieces.title: MEMORY METRO
pieces.subtitle: (coming soon!)
overview: Memory Metro blends math fluency practice with genuine working-memory training. Players route trains through a growing transit map while holding and updating numbers in their head — the difficulty adapts to keep each learner at the edge of their capacity.
Designed and built solo with Claude Code, from the game loop and difficulty model down to the art direction.

contact.heading: contact
contact.blurb: Building something for learners or teachers? I'd love to hear about it.
